shortTitle: Your life as Every Rank of Vibe Coder

content.text: You walk into your first day as a Level 1 Vibe Coder without knowing what it means to hold the title.
content.imageDescription: A nervous new hire stands in the doorway of a modern tech office, holding a plastic lanyard and bright badge. The room is filled with open desks, potted plants, monitors, and people typing or chatting.

content.text: The badge is just a bright sticker, a plastic lanyard, and someone else’s playlist humming through the open-plan room.
content.imageDescription: Close-up of a hand clutching a shiny sticker badge and plastic lanyard, with blurry office desks in the background. A music speaker sits nearby, softly glowing.

content.text: You watch blinking cursors and hear laughter you can’t quite join yet.
content.imageDescription: Over-the-shoulder shot: the main character sees computer screens with blinking text cursors and coworkers grouped together, laughing, slightly out of focus.

content.text: You tell yourself it’s just a job, but you feel the ground of expectation shifting beneath every footstep.
content.imageDescription: Low-angle shot of the protagonist walking hesitantly across the office floor, their shadow elongated. The ground appears subtly uneven, suggesting uncertainty.

content.text: Your first pull request gets accepted—one line change, a typo—but the chat fills with digital high-fives.
content.imageDescription: A computer monitor displays a code editor with a green 'pull request accepted' message. Onscreen chat bubbles pop with emoji high-fives and congratulations around the screen.

content.text: For a moment, you believe you belong.
content.imageDescription: Medium shot of the main character smiling in front of their computer, posture relaxed, surrounded by a faint glow of acceptance in an otherwise busy office.

content.text: Then a ticket lands in your queue: three sentences, two requirements, one uncertainty that gnaws at you after dark.
content.imageDescription: A digital task board shows a highlighted ticket, marked urgent. The character reads it with a furrowed brow, the office lights dimming outside the window.

content.text: The silence between questions grows longer.
content.imageDescription: Side view of the protagonist hesitating at their desk, hands hovering over the keyboard, the workspace around them slightly blurred in quiet.

content.text: You read old threads, relearn what you thought you understood, panic quietly as the weekend creeps closer.
content.imageDescription: Night view: the character at their desk, face lit by monitor, surrounded by sticky notes, open tabs, and a half-empty coffee cup. Calendar on screen shows the weekend approaching.

content.text: You hit submit just before midnight. It’s enough—for now.
content.imageDescription: A close-up of a finger clicking the 'submit' button on a computer screen. Clock in background shows just before midnight.

content.text: Promotion to Level 2 feels less like a leap, more like stepping onto thin ice.
content.imageDescription: Office setting: character receives a new badge, stepping carefully as if on fragile ice, uncertainty visible in their posture.

content.text: You have a title now. Junior Developer. Your code runs in production. Errors, when they come, are public. Your name sits beside them, a quiet testament.
content.imageDescription: A screen displays a production error log with the character’s name next to a highlighted error, visible to the whole team on a shared dashboard.

content.text: You spend most days fixing other people’s bugs, wishing you were writing something new.
content.imageDescription: The protagonist sits tired at their desk, surrounded by sticky notes marked 'bug', glancing out a window as unfinished code glows on their monitor.

content.text: When you finally crack a tricky data flow, the surge is instant—one burst of clarity in hours of staring.
content.imageDescription: Close-up of the character’s face lighting up with joy, eyes wide, as a flowchart or code solution becomes clear on their screen.

content.text: You ride that wave straight through until doubt creeps back in.
content.imageDescription: The same character, now sitting back with a thoughtful expression, euphoria fading, uncertainty returning.

content.text: When the review comes, your mentor points out things you missed. "Don’t worry," they say. Everyone misses these at first. You still do.
content.imageDescription: Mentor and protagonist sit side-by-side at a workstation, mentor gently pointing to the screen while reassuring the new coder.

content.text: You start second-guessing your habits. You start rewriting comments late at night, catching yourself before you send.
content.imageDescription: Dimly lit home workspace: the protagonist stares thoughtfully at a glowing monitor, fingers poised over keyboard, draft comments visible, a hand pausing before hitting 'send'.

content.text: One quiet Tuesday, a teammate you admire merges your code without a single note.
content.imageDescription: A computer screen shows a notification: 'Merged with no comments.' Reflected in the display is the protagonist’s proud, astonished face.

content.text: That night, you sleep like you’ve finished a marathon.
content.imageDescription: Shot of the protagonist peacefully asleep in bed, badge and lanyard on the nightstand, moonlight streaming through the window.

content.text: Level 3, Developer. The ranks thin out. Expectations double.
content.imageDescription: Wide shot of a smaller team at longer desks, with more serious expressions. The protagonist’s badge shows ‘Level 3’. Distant figures fade, indicating fewer peers.

content.text: The new hires look up to you, but you still feel unguarded, exposed.
content.imageDescription: Mid-shot showing a couple of eager new hires watching the protagonist, who appears slightly anxious and vulnerable at their desk.

content.text: Stakes are bigger: deployments, postmortems, standing up in front of the team with nothing prepared except your voice and a few slides.
content.imageDescription: The protagonist stands nervously at the front of a conference room, slides projected behind, facing attentive teammates.

content.text: There are days everything clicks, days when abstractions tumble out of your mind onto the screen, almost effortlessly.
content.imageDescription: Medium shot of the character in creative flow, fingers flying over the keyboard, lines of code cascading onto the monitor.

content.text: There are just as many stretches where nothing connects. Standups blur.
content.imageDescription: Bored, unfocused protagonist in a video call or daily standup meeting, colleagues’ faces blurred or repetitive in the interface.

content.text: You reread your own code and don’t recognize yourself in it.
content.imageDescription: Close-up of code on screen, main character’s confused reflection visible, as if searching for familiarity in their work.

content.text: The cycle—tension, release, learning, regret—spins faster.
content.imageDescription: Impression of a calendar or clock spinning rapidly behind the protagonist, who alternates between tense, relieved, and thoughtful expressions.

content.text: But you teach someone else a trick, watch their eyes light up, and remember: you can do this.
content.imageDescription: The main character shows a coding trick on screen to a younger teammate, who lights up with excitement and gratitude.

content.text: Maybe they can too.
content.imageDescription: Close-up of the mentee, smiling confidently as they try the trick themselves on their own laptop.

content.text: Senior developer, Level 4, is different. People come to you for answers.
content.imageDescription: The main character, with a Level 4 badge, sits at a desk while several colleagues line up or gather around, seeking advice.

content.text: You’ve traded imposter syndrome for responsibility, or so you tell yourself.
content.imageDescription: Close-up: protagonist looks at their reflection in a computer screen, face a mix of confidence and lingering doubt.

content.text: A big launch goes live. Something breaks. You are the first call. Your chest tightens, fingers already tracing logs for a root cause.
content.imageDescription: Late night: the protagonist, tense and focused, scrolls rapidly through error logs on a large screen, phone ringing beside them.

content.text: You fix the fire, then stay up running simulations to be sure it won’t happen again.
content.imageDescription: Character at their computer, multiple windows open showing simulations and graphs, coffee cup nearby, the city still dark outside.

content.text: Praise feels heavier now. Criticism echoes.
content.imageDescription: The character stands surrounded by coworkers; some clap, others critique, their words visually echoing around the protagonist’s head.

content.text: You mentor, you advocate, sometimes you mediate when no one else will.
content.imageDescription: The main character mediates a tense conversation between two feuding colleagues, hands open in a gesture of calm.

content.text: You wonder if you’re leading or just barely out in front.
content.imageDescription: Wide shot of the protagonist walking slightly ahead of their team down a corridor, looking back uncertainly over their shoulder.

content.text: There are quiet victories and quieter doubts—and you keep showing up.
content.imageDescription: The protagonist sits alone in a quiet office at dusk, a small smile on their face, light from the monitor casting a gentle glow.

content.text: Lead coder, the final badge. When you speak, people pause.
content.imageDescription: Front view: the protagonist, now with a distinguished badge, addresses a conference table full of attentive teammates who all pause to listen.

content.text: You’re shaping the architecture now, seeing the system as a whole, making impossible calls between competing priorities.
content.imageDescription: Overhead shot: character sketches a complex software architecture diagram on a whiteboard, pinning sticky notes in a web of dependencies.

content.text: Every small win contains a dozen invisible tradeoffs.
content.imageDescription: The protagonist considers a completed project board, each ticket balanced with faint, ghost-like tickets representing unseen tradeoffs.

content.text: There’s pride in watching a project soar, knowing you pushed it toward lift.
content.imageDescription: The character gazes at a monitor displaying soaring usage graphs or growth curves for a successful product launch, swelling with pride.

content.text: But every decision leaves ghosts—the path not taken, the voice you overruled, the bug that got through anyway.
content.imageDescription: The protagonist sits quietly in a darkened office, shadowed shapes representing past choices and regrets drifting just beyond the glow of their desk lamp.

content.text: In the quiet after the sprint, you look back at the younger you, the one clutching a sticker badge, and realize the gaps that were once wide are just seams now, barely visible, stitched by effort and time.
content.imageDescription: A dreamy overlay: present-day protagonist glances pensively at a faded vision of their younger self in the same chair, holding the original sticker badge.

content.text: You aren’t the same, but you wouldn’t trade the cost, or the change, for anything else.
content.imageDescription: Final frame: the main character stands confidently at a window overlooking the city at sunset, serene and content, badge gleaming on their lanyard.
